project_overview.project_name: Public-Private Partnerships and Public Investment Advisory Project (PPIAP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To develop a pipeline of feasible private and publicly funded projects
project_overview.total_financing: US$50 million (IDA Grant: US$20 million; ARTF Grant: US$30 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Government institutions (MoF, CPA, line ministries), private investors (large, medium, small), and the people of Afghanistan through improved allocation of public resources and infrastructure development

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Afghanistan is designated as a fragile state. The project explicitly addresses FCV context with ongoing security challenges, insurgency, and conflict affecting implementation and investment climate

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Strengthening Institutional and Technical Capacity
key_components.description: Development of PIM-PPP framework, standardized project appraisal documents, PPP Law Rules of Procedure, review and revision of relevant laws, FCCL framework, resident advisers to CPA, capacity-building programs, and awareness-building campaigns

Establishment and operation of IPPF with two windows: (a) Appraisal Window for pre-feasibility and feasibility studies for public/PPP projects; (b) PPP Project Development Fund Window for feasibility studies, safeguards documents, detailed design and tender documents for PPP projects
key_components.financing: US$40.0 million (US$15 million IDA; US$25 million ARTF)

key_components.description: Support for Project Management Unit (PMU) operations including monitoring, management, and implementation obligations until mainstreamed into Afghan civil service

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Central Partnership Authority (CPA) within Ministry of Finance (MoF), with support from MoF Procurement Directorate and Finance Directorate
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: High-level oversight through Development Councils (DCs); centralized coordination through MoFBD, MoFPD, CPA, and MoEc; Project Evaluation Committee and Project Recommendation Committee for project review; line ministries retain responsibility for project identification and implementation
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for Borrowers; prior review threshold for high-risk implementing agency; use of Standard Procurement Documents (SPDs), Requests for Proposals (RFPs); STEP system for tracking; procurement through open national competition; transaction advisers for PPP-specific procurement
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: CPA conducts M&E with dedicated M&E specialist; semiannual and annual monitoring reports; annual and semiannual M&E reviews; midterm review at 2 years; implementation support missions at least twice yearly; monitoring of safeguards compliance; baseline data collection and stakeholder surveys

identified_risks.risk_description: Power-sharing arrangements under National Unity Government create tension; potential changes in political support for PPPs; increased insurgency weakening government capacity; poor governance and corruption risks; blurred relationship between business and politics
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government's continued effort to strengthen legal framework and address corruption; alignment with ANPDF reform agenda; integration with budget circular process; development of clear institutional and legal arrangements

identified_risks.risk_description: Heavy reliance on aid; reduction in security and civilian support would pressure fiscal sustainability; constrained fiscal space with revenues at 11.4% of GDP financing only 60% of recurrent budget; stagnating economic growth at 2.6% (2017) and projected 2.2% (2018)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project promotes maximization of private financing for development; supports structural reforms; integration of fiscal sustainability considerations into decision-making; FCCL framework to manage contingent liabilities

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak enforcement of regulatory, policy, and legal framework; lack of in-house GoIRA technical expertise; nonavailability of technical experts on PPPs; multisectoral coordination challenges; feasibility studies may not yield anticipated results; lack of comprehensive development plans
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance to streamline PPP framework; alignment with PIM framework; capacity building and institutional strengthening; development of manuals and guidelines; revision of relevant laws and policies

identified_risks.risk_description: PPP concept is relatively new in Afghanistan; complex technical challenges in PPP preparation; Afghanistan's fragility may reduce private investor interest; Government's fiscal constraints inhibit ability to guarantee PPPs; success depends on availability of donor funding for guarantees, insurance, capital subsidies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Robust preparation of PPP projects through upstream identification and prioritization; strong synergies with other World Bank and donor-funded projects; support for risk identification and mitigation along project cycle; availability of World Bank, IFC, and MIGA guarantees and insurance

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Highly uneven and thinly spread technical capacity in public sector; long-term aid dependency creating vulnerability to delays and coordination problems; CPA is new agency with limited experience; limited capacity in line ministries
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong emphasis on institutional strengthening within MoF and sponsoring line ministries; resident advisers to work closely with CPA and line ministries; capacity-building programs; IPPF support for capacity building at MoFPD, CPA, and line ministries; train-the-trainer approach

identified_risks.risk_description: No budget allocated for Project in FY2018; framework/guidelines for accessing IPPF funding not yet developed; Financial Management Manual not yet prepared; limited FM team capacity; high procurement risk implementing agency; limited familiarity with PPP transactions
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Preparation of Financial Management Manual by June 30, 2018; in-year budget approval from President; augmentation of FM team with one assistant; prior review threshold for high-risk implementing agency; hiring of transaction advisers for PPP advisory services; use of World Bank SPDs and procurement procedures; STEP system implementation; internal audit semiannually by MoF Internal Audit Department

identified_risks.risk_description: Project classified as Category A; some prepared projects may trigger safeguard risks including land acquisition and resettlement; potential impacts on natural habitats, international waterways, dams; environmental and social safeguards staff capacity limited
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF) and Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared and disclosed; social screening and scoping at pre-feasibility stage; Social Impact Assessment and site-specific Social Management Plans at feasibility stage; RAPs where needed; hiring of environmental and social safeguards specialists in MoF; riparian notification for international waterways projects; consultation with stakeholders

identified_risks.risk_description: Implementation relies on productive collaboration between multiple stakeholders (MoFPD, CPA, line ministries, private sector); number of stakeholders may lead to competition for resources and complicate decision-making; coordination challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project designed with budget for enhanced coordination and communication strategy; MoF has experience implementing World Bank projects; Development Councils provide high-level oversight; clear institutional roles and responsibilities defined; regular coordination meetings and PPD events

identified_risks.risk_description: Afghanistan still recovering from conflict; recent violence in areas where infrastructure needs to be built; continuing high security risks; 2,640 people killed and 5,380 injured between January-September 2017; 200,000+ internally displaced in first half of 2017; 296,000+ refugees returned; security affecting business and consumer confidence
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project design accounts for fragile context; focus on preparation rather than implementation; flexibility in implementation arrangements; coordination with security assessments; integration with broader development agenda; support for institutional strengthening to improve government capacity

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Grievance Redress Committee to be established in MoF; exploration of feasibility of central grievance hotline integrating existing project-level hotlines; support for review and upgrade of grievance arrangements; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; World Bank Inspection Panel available for complaints
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender analysis to be conducted for potential sectors; feasibility studies to use gender tagging tools; all development of policies, regulations, and manuals to be gender-informed; public-private dialogue and capacity-building to be inclusive with special provisions for women and majority-women-owned SMEs; training for female civil servants; capacity-building for female entrepreneurs; consultations with women beneficiaries through Afghan Women Chamber of Commerce; target of 5 female staff among 25 CP3P certified staff; all feasibility studies and PPP projects to consider gender issues
vulnerable_groups: Poor and impoverished population (poverty increased from 38.3% in 2011 to 54.5% in 2016/17)
Unemployed and underemployed (unemployment at 23.9%; 400,000 new workers entering workforce annually)
Internally displaced persons (200,000+ displaced in first half of 2017)
Refugees (296,000+ returned from Iran and Pakistan)
Women (limited participation, unequal legal rights, limited access to finance, barriers to business registration)
Communities affected by land acquisition and resettlement from infrastructure projects

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed for fragile, conflict, and violence (FCV) context; recognizes that PPP transactions in FCV contexts are complex and require long lead time; acknowledges security risks affecting project implementation and achievement of results; incorporates lessons from World Bank experience in FCV environments; focuses on preparation rather than implementation to reduce security exposure; integrates conflict sensitivity into institutional strengthening and capacity building; supports development of enabling environment for private investment despite fragility
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Afghanistan designated as fragile state; key fragility factors include: (1) ongoing insurgency and conflict with 2,640 deaths and 5,380 injuries in first 9 months of 2017; (2) large-scale displacement (200,000+ IDPs in H1 2017, 296,000+ refugee returns); (3) severe poverty (54.5% in 2016/17, up from 38.3% in 2011); (4) high unemployment (23.9%) and limited productive employment; (5) stagnating economic growth (2.6% in 2017, projected 2.2% in 2018); (6) heavy aid dependency (17% of GDP); (7) weak institutional capacity and governance; (8) limited private sector development; (9) power-sharing tensions in National Unity Government; (10) corruption and governance challenges
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Continuing high security risks have potential to adversely affect project implementation and achievement of results; recent violence involves opposing forces in areas where infrastructure needs to be built; security affecting business and consumer confidence; project design mitigates security risks by focusing on preparation activities rather than implementation; implementation support missions and coordination mechanisms account for security constraints; flexibility built into implementation arrangements to accommodate security situation changes
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Project triggered Involuntary Resettlement (OP/BP 4.12) because some public and PPP projects prepared under Component 2, if subsequently implemented outside project scope, could result in land acquisition and resettlement; Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) prepared with draft Terms of Reference for Resettlement Action Plans; due diligence procedures for land acquisition and resettlement included in RPF; preliminary social screening and scoping at pre-feasibility stage; Social Impact Assessment and site-specific Social Management Plans at feasibility stage; RAPs to be prepared where needed; local disclosure and consultation required per national law and World Bank safeguards policies. Context: 200,000+ internally displaced persons in first half of 2017 due to conflict; 296,000+ refugees returned from Iran and Pakistan in same period
